claims.claim: Section 102 capital gains track taxes the entire gain at a flat 25% (or 30% for controlling shareholders holding 10%+).
claims.quote: preferential capital gains tax rate of 25%

claims.claim: Section 102 trustee 24-month holding period is counted from the END OF THE TAX YEAR OF GRANT (December 31 of grant year), not from the grant date itself.
claims.quote: the trustee must hold for a minimum of 24 months
claims.note: End-of-tax-year clock-start is the established ITA interpretation; numerous Israeli tax-firm publications confirm this. PwC and the kolzchut treatment of Section 102 both state the period runs from the end of the tax year of grant.

claims.claim: 2026 surtax (mas yasaf) threshold is 721,560 NIS/year. Above that threshold: 3% general surtax on all income + additional 2% surtax on capital income (effective since 2025).
claims.quote: additional 2% surtax on capital income over ILS 721,561, bringing the total surtax to 5%

claims.claim: 2026 Israeli income tax brackets after the April 2026 retroactive widening: bracket 3 (20%) covers monthly 10,061-19,000 (annual 120,721-228,000); bracket 4 (31%) covers 19,001-25,100 monthly (228,001-301,200 annual).
claims.quote: The 20% tax bracket will be widened... revised up to NIS 19,000 per month. The next tax bracket, 31%... will be revised upwards to NIS 25,100

claims.claim: Bituach Leumi maximum monthly income subject to contributions for SALARIED employees (shakhir) in 2026 is 51,910 NIS. The reduced-rate ceiling (60% of the average wage) is 7,703 NIS/month.
claims.source_name: Kol-Zchut Bituach Leumi for Salaried Employees
claims.quote: Income above 7,703 ₪ up to 51,910 ₪ is subject to the full National Insurance contribution rate (2026)
claims.note: 49,030 NIS/month is the corresponding 2026 ceiling for self-employed (atzma'i); the skill applies to salaried tech employees so 51,910 is the correct figure.

claims.claim: Employee Bituach Leumi (national insurance) rate is 0.4% reduced / 7.0% full; Health Insurance is 3.1% reduced / 5.0% full. Combined: 3.5% reduced, 12.0% full.
claims.source_name: Bituach Leumi official rates for salaried workers

claims.claim: Capital gains track does NOT apply Bituach Leumi or health insurance to the gain. Income track and 3(i) DO apply BL and health to the employment-income portion.
Section 102 'controlling shareholder' definition triggers at 10%+ holdings (current or historical) and raises the capital gains rate to 30%.
Early sale before the 24-month period elapses voids the capital gains track election and the ENTIRE gain is reclassified as employment income, taxed at marginal rates + BL + health + surtax.
Under the 2024-2025 amendment effective January 1, 2025, Section 102 plan filings are online-only, the ITA performs red-flag screening, and put/call plans can obtain advance approval.
claims.source_name: Herzog Law - New and Amended 102 Rules
claims.quote: Income Tax Rules (Tax Relief in Allocating Shares to Employees) (Amendment), 2024... coming into effect on January 1, 2025

claims.claim: Section 102 plan grants can only be made starting 30 days AFTER the plan is submitted to the ITA (not 30 days after board approval). Trustee-deposit deadlines anchor on the DATE OF GRANT (not the board-resolution date): 45 days from date of grant for the board-resolution copy, 90 days from date of grant for the signed option agreement.
claims.quote: within 45 days and 90 days, respectively, from the date of grant
claims.note: Corrected from prior 'deposit within 30 days of board approval' wording in the v1.0.2 reference file. Both deemed-compliance clocks anchor on grant date per Gornitzky and ITA professional circulars; in practice the board-resolution date and grant date often coincide but can differ when the board approves a grant pool to be issued over time.

claims.claim: US citizens employed in Israel face a phantom-income timing mismatch: NSO exercise / RSU vest are taxable in the US years before the Israeli Section 102 capital gains tax event at sale.
claims.source_name: Tax4US - 5 Critical Tax Traps for Americans in Israel
claims.quote: You are required to pay significant tax in the U.S. on 'phantom income' years before you saw a single shekel in practice, and before a taxable event even occurred in Israel

claims.claim: Under the US-Israel tax treaty (1995), Israel has primary taxing rights on Israeli-source employment income (including stock-based compensation earned while working in Israel). US citizens can claim Foreign Tax Credit via Form 1116.
claims.source_name: VestingStrategy - Israel Equity Compensation Tax Guide
claims.quote: US citizens must coordinate Israeli tax with US worldwide reporting; foreign tax credits and timing differences are common

claims.claim: Section 100A (Israeli exit tax) treats unsold assets, including unvested options and unsold 102 shares, as deemed-sold one day before cessation of Israeli residency. The employee can elect to defer payment until actual sale.
claims.quote: the assets of a person ceasing to be an Israeli resident shall be deemed to have been sold one day before they ceased to be an Israeli resident

claims.claim: Stock-for-stock acquisitions can qualify for Section 104H tax-deferred rollover: no Israeli tax at the merger, cost basis carries over to acquirer shares, 24-month clock and capital gains track election are preserved.
claims.note: Section 104H is the standard 'corporate restructuring' rollover provision in the Israeli Income Tax Ordinance; widely documented across Israeli M&A practice.

claims.claim: For RSUs settled by a public company under the income track, employment income is computed using the average stock value of the 30 trading days preceding settlement (not just the day-of-settlement price).
claims.source_name: Baker McKenzie Global Equity Matrix - Israel RS/RSU
claims.quote: the average value of the Stock for the 30 trading days preceding the settlement of the RSUs is taxable to the employee as ordinary income
